commonProblems.h2: BMW X6 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: BMW X6 N57 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW X6 N57 Timing Chain Failure - Why Pre-2015 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive30d, xDrive35d, xDrive40d (2008-2015, N57D30A & N57D30B)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine block, running through oil galleries that become blocked by sludge from extended service intervals or poor oil quality. The chain stretches, guides crack, and eventually the chain snaps - causing catastrophic valve and piston contact.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Replace before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit, tensioners, guides, sprockets, oil pump chain, sump gasket
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles - only viable if engine is running without fault codes or rattles

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Post-Failure Chain & Head Rebuild - Chain snapped, no piston damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain replacement plus cylinder head rebuild, valve replacement, head gasket, full timing kit
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - cost effective if bore scoring is absent

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Catastrophic valve/piston damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine (upgraded chain components), new gaskets, oil pump, water pump, fitted
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles on rebuild - includes 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: If your N57 X6 is under 100,000 miles and you hear chain rattle on cold start, preventative replacement at a specialist is the smart move - it costs half what a dealer charges and avoids engine destruction. Once the chain has snapped, replacement is almost always better value than rebuilding a damaged cylinder head. On a 2008-2015 X6 worth £4,000-£10,000, a specialist rebuilt engine at £4,500-£7,500 fitted gives you a warranty and upgraded components that the factory never offered.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N57D30 engine prices for your BMW X6

problems.h4: BMW X6 EGR Cooler Failure - Diesel Coolant Loss & Misfire Diagnosis
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive30d, xDrive35d, xDrive40d (2008-2019, N57D30A, N57D30B & B57D30B)
problems.rootCause: The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) cooler develops internal cracks due to thermal cycling between 700°C exhaust gases and engine coolant. Coolant leaks into the intake manifold, causing misfires, white smoke, and eventually hydrolock if enough coolant enters a cylinder.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cooler Replacement - Cracked cooler, no hydrolock
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, new gaskets, cooling system flush, intake manifold inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent if using genuine BMW or upgraded cooler - 2-3 hour job

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cooler + DPF Cleaning - Coolant contamination of exhaust system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR cooler, DPF removal and professional cleaning, EGR valve, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full emissions system - 1-2 years before DPF may need replacement

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Hydrolock bent conrods
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded cooler, new injectors if water damaged, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Only required if engine hydrolocked - rare, but catastrophic

problems.recommendation: An EGR cooler failure caught early is a £550-£850 specialist fix - don't ignore the low coolant warning light. If you've already driven through misfires, get a compression test before replacing anything. On a high-mileage X6 (120,000+), if the cooler has failed twice, consider fitting an aftermarket upgraded cooler or an EGR delete map (where MOT-legal). Never let a dealer sell you an engine for EGR cooler failure without a compression test first.
problems.cta: Compare BMW X6 EGR cooler replacement quotes from UK specialists

problems.h4: BMW X6 N63 Oil Consumption - Valve Stem Seal & PCV System Failure
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive50i, ActiveHybrid (2008-2019, N63B44A, N63B44B, N63B44C)
problems.rootCause: The hot-vee design cooks valve stem seals, causing oil to leak into combustion chambers. Simultaneously, the PCV system fails, drawing oil vapour into the intake. Consumption of 1 litre per 600 miles is common - at 1 litre per 300 miles, catalytic converter damage is imminent.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Valve Stem Seal Replacement - Oil consumption under 1L/600 miles
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve stem seals (upgraded Viton material), camshaft locking tools, timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - requires specialist tooling, 2-3 day job

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Top End Rebuild - 1L/300-400 miles
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve stem seals, PCV system, turbo coolant lines, injector seals, cam cover gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles - addresses all oil consumption sources

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Catastrophic catalyst failure or scoring
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt N63 with upgraded seals, revised PCV, new turbos if damaged, fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Only when repair exceeds replacement value - X5/X6 N63 values are £6,000-£15,000

problems.recommendation: On a 2008-2014 X6 xDrive50i worth £6,000-£12,000, valve stem seal replacement at a specialist (£2,200-£3,500) is almost always the right call - it halves dealer pricing and uses upgraded Viton seals. Only go to full replacement if the engine has been run very low on oil (causing bore scoring) or if catalytic converters are already destroyed. Never pay a dealer for a full engine on a car worth under £10,000 - specialist rebuilds offer the same warranty for 30-40% less.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N63B44 engine prices for your BMW X6

problems.h4: BMW X6 Turbo Actuator Failure - Limp Mode & Boost Loss on Diesel & Petrol
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive30d, xDrive35d, xDrive35i, xDrive40d (2008-2019, N57 & N55 engines)
problems.rootCause: The vacuum-actuated turbo wastegate or variable geometry turbo (VGT) actuator motors fail due to carbon buildup or electronic wear. Fault codes P2263 (turbo actuator) or P0243 (wastegate solenoid) appear, triggering limp mode with reduced power.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Replacement Only - VGT actuator failure, turbo intact
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VGT actuator, recalibrate with diagnostic tool, road test
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - actuator is serviceable part, turbo not removed

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Rebuild - Seized actuator caused turbo damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild with new actuator, bearings, seals, VGT ring
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles on rebuild - cost effective if turbo is otherwise sound

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - New or Rebuilt Turbo - Catastrophic turbine/shaft failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete new or factory-rebuilt turbocharger, actuator, oil/water lines, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent - 12-month warranty on replacement unit

problems.recommendation: Limp mode on an X6 is often just a failed actuator, not a dead turbo. A specialist with diagnostic tools can confirm in 30 minutes. Actuator-only replacement at £500-£850 is the smart first step - don't let a garage sell you a full turbo unless the shaft has play or the turbine is damaged. On N57 diesels, check the oil supply line filter (banjo bolt) at the same time - it blocks and kills turbos repeatedly.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW X6 turbo actuator repair or replacement

problems.group: BMW X6 DPF Blockage & Regeneration Failure
problems.h4: BMW X6 DPF Blockage & Regeneration Failure - Diesel Exhaust System Collapse
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive30d, xDrive35d, xDrive40d, M50d (2010-2019, N57D30B & B57D30B)
problems.rootCause: Repeated short journeys prevent passive DPF regeneration. Ash builds up beyond the ECU's ability to force-regenerate. Eventually, back pressure damages the turbo, cracks the exhaust manifold, or causes engine oil dilution from failed regeneration attempts.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Forced DPF Regeneration - Warning light only, no permanent damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic forced regeneration, motorway test drive, differential pressure sensor check
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary - works only if ash load is below 80%

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Removal & Professional Cleaning - Ash load over 80%, no turbo damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, flow bench clean to factory spec, replace pressure sensor, regen
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 miles - effective but not permanent on high-mileage cars

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - DPF + Turbo Inspection - Back pressure damaged turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New DPF (genuine or high-quality aftermarket), turbo inspection/rebuild, oil change, regen
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent for DPF - turbo longevity depends on damage severity

problems.recommendation: If your X6 is a town car doing under 15,000 miles per year, a DPF cleaning every 2-3 years is cheaper than an engine replacement. Once you see "Drivetrain malfunction - continued driving possible", get a forced regeneration immediately - ignoring it will kill the turbo. On an X6 worth under £8,000, a £450-£750 cleaning is sensible; over £8,000, consider a new DPF if you plan to keep the car for 3+ years. Never buy an X6 diesel that has done only urban miles without a DPF ash report.
problems.cta: Compare BMW X6 DPF cleaning and replacement quotes

problems.h4: BMW X6 Valvetronic Motor Failure - Petrol Engine Starting & Idle Issues
problems.affectedModels: BMW X6 xDrive35i, xDrive50i (2010-2019, N55B30 & N63B44B)
problems.rootCause: The Valvetronic electric motor that controls intake valve lift wears out, or its eccentric shaft sensor fails. The engine defaults to a limp-home throttle mode, causing rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, and eventually failure to start.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Valvetronic Motor Replacement - Fault codes, engine still runs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace Valvetronic motor, re-learn end stops with diagnostic tool, clear adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - motor is the common failure point, not the shaft

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Motor + Eccentric Shaft - Shaft worn or sensor failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace motor, eccentric shaft, sensor, valve cover gasket (removed for access)
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles - shaft wear is rare before 120,000 miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Valvetronic Delete Map - Permanent solution option
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Custom ECU map deleting Valvetronic, reverting to throttle-based control
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent - not MOT-legal on Euro 6 cars, but effective on 2008-2014 models

problems.recommendation: On a 2010-2014 X6 xDrive35i, a Valvetronic motor replacement at a specialist for £700-£1,200 is straightforward - the motor fails, not the shaft. Don't let a garage sell you an eccentric shaft unless they've diagnosed it with BMW ISTA software. On older, high-mileage cars, a Valvetronic delete map (where MOT-legal) is a permanent fix that costs less than the motor alone - but confirm emissions compliance first.
problems.cta: Compare BMW X6 Valvetronic repair and engine quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW X6
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing N57 timing chain failure, N63 oil consumption, or a blocked DPF on your diesel X6, the cost of repair or replacement doesn't have to write off your car. EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK BMW X6 specialists - all of whom supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to prevent the original failure from recurring. With 12-month unlimited mileage warranties, nationwide delivery, and fitting available across the UK, you can get back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW X6 Engine Replacement Prices Now →

engineSizes.h2: BMW X6 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: BMW X6 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel is the dominant engine across all three X6 generations, accounting for the majority of UK sales. From the single-turbo N57D30A in the early E71 xDrive30d to the quad-cam triple-turbo N57D30S in the F16 M50d and the latest B57D30C with 48V mild hybrid assist in the G06, this displacement defines X6 diesel ownership. Power ranges from 235hp to 381hp depending on turbo configuration and generation, with torque always exceeding 500Nm - making it well-suited to the X6's substantial kerb weight.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain wear on pre-2015 N57 units (80,000-120,000 miles)
EGR cooler coolant leaks across all generations
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X6 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X6 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre straight-six petrol first appeared in the E71 xDrive35i with the twin-turbo N54B30, later replaced by the more reliable single twin-scroll N55B30 in the F16 generation. For the G06 xDrive40i, BMW introduced the B58B30 - a closed-deck design with integrated exhaust manifold that produces 340hp in standard form. While less common than diesel X6s in the UK, the 3.0-litre petrol offers smoother power delivery and lower entry prices on the used market.
commonFailurePoints: coolant leaks from expansion tank or pump (B58)
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X6 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.4-litre V8 is the flagship petrol option for X6 owners demanding maximum power. The N63B44A in the E71 xDrive50i introduced BMW's hot-vee turbo arrangement but suffered from chronic oil consumption through valve stem seal failure. Revisions with N63B44B (F16) improved durability, while the S63B44 variants in X6 M and X6 M Competition (E71, F16, G06) added forged internals, upgraded turbos and a cross-bank exhaust manifold for outputs up to 625hp. The latest G06 M60i uses the N63B44E with 48V mild hybrid assistance.
commonFailurePoints: Valve stem seal failure causing 1L/600-mile oil consumption (N63)
rod bearing wear under high load (S63)
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 4.4L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW X6 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW X6 3.0L Plug-in Hybrid Engines
items.description: The xDrive45e (G06, 2020-present) pairs the B58B30 3.0-litre petrol engine with a 113hp electric motor and 24kWh battery pack, delivering 394hp combined and up to 54 miles of electric-only range. This powertrain offers the lowest benefit-in-kind (BIK) rate for company car drivers while maintaining the X6's performance credentials. The hybrid system uses an eight-speed ZF automatic with an integrated electric motor replacing the torque converter.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X6 3.0L plug-in hybrid engine availability

items.title: BMW X6 4.4L Mild Hybrid Engines
items.description: The latest G06 X6 M60i (2023-present) and G06 X6 M Competition receive 48V mild hybrid assistance integrated into the ZF eight-speed transmission. The system provides 12hp boost under acceleration, smoother stop-start operation, and coasting functionality at motorway speeds. While not a full hybrid, this technology reduces turbo lag and improves fuel economy by approximately 8% compared to non-hybrid N63 units. Not sure which engine size your BMW X6 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK BMW X6 specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X6 4.4L mild hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: BMW X6 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every engine change across the BMW X6’s production life (2008 to present), helping you identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The E71 X6 launched BMW’s ‘Sports Activity Coupé’ concept with a full range of turbocharged six and V8 engines.
keyChanges: All-new E71 chassis (2008-2014), shared with X5 E70 platform
Sequential twin-turbo diesel introduced for xDrive35d
ActiveHybrid V8 model added to range in 2010
First BMW to offer twin-turbo V8 in SAV segment
knownFor: Bold coupé-SUV styling that created an entirely new segment
N54 twin-turbo petrol known for HPFP failures
N57 diesel timing chain issues emerging on high-mileage cars
Heavy vehicle (over 2,100kg) demands robust engine specification
years.cta: Compare quotes for 2008-2010 BMW X6 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW X6 xDrive30d engine replacement • BMW X6 N57 timing chain repair • BMW X6 2009 engine cost
enquiries: BMW X6 N57 timing chain repair

years.preview: E71 LCI: N57 Update & ActiveHybrid Arrives
years.description: The E71 facelift brought the N57D30B diesel with higher output and introduced the xDrive40d badge.
keyChanges: N57D30B replaces earlier diesel variants with 306hp output
xDrive40d debuts (twin-turbo 3.0L diesel, 306hp)
ActiveHybrid X6 launched with N63 V8 + electric motor
M50d not yet available - top diesel remained 40d
N55B30 petrol replaced N54 in later cars
knownFor: N57D30B considered more reliable than earlier N57 variants
ActiveHybrid rare (only 2010-2014) - complex hybrid system
EGR cooler failures become common on diesel variants
S63 V8 X6 M available from 2010 (covered separately below)
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2014 BMW X6 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW X6 xDrive40d engine replacement • BMW X6 ActiveHybrid battery cost • BMW X6 2011 engine N57D30B price

years.preview: E71 X6 M: S63 V8 Performance Flagship
years.description: The first X6 M arrived in 2010, packing a twin-turbo 4.4L V8 producing 555hp and transforming the X6 into a genuine performance machine.
keyChanges: X6 M (E71) launched as 2010 model year
S63B44A engine shared with X5 M (E70)
M-specific chassis tuning, suspension, and cooling
Produced 408kW (547hp) - up to 600hp with competition package
knownFor: First M-badged SAV/coupé-SUV from BMW M division
Rod bearing wear a documented concern on high-mileage examples
Turbo coolant line and gasket failures common
Retains strong enthusiast following despite running costs

faq.h2: BMW X6 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW X6 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A BMW X6 engine replacement typically costs £4,500 to £8,000 including supply and fitting, depending on the engine code, variant and whether you choose a used, reconditioned or rebuilt unit.
keyPoints: Used BMW X6 engines: £3,000 - £5,000 supply only
Reconditioned BMW X6 engines: £2,000 - £3,500 supply only
Rebuilt BMW X6 engines: £4,000 - £6,000 supply only
Professional fitting adds £1,000 - £2,000 including ECU coding
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 engine replacement costs from UK specialists

items.question: Is the BMW X6 N57 diesel engine reliable? I keep hearing about timing chain failure.
items.answer: The N57 diesel engine in the X6 is powerful and smooth, but timing chain failure on pre-2015 models is a genuine risk - typically between 80,000 and 120,000 miles.
keyPoints: N57 timing chain rattle is the first warning sign - address immediately
Preventative chain replacement costs far less than a full engine rebuild
Post-2015 B57 diesel engines have improved chain design and fewer failures
Some owners report complete engine failure without prior warning symptoms
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X6 N57 engine replacement with warranty

items.question: What are the most common BMW X6 engine problems by generation?
items.answer: The E71 (2008-2014) suffers N57 timing chain and N63 oil consumption issues. The F16 (2015-2019) improved reliability but EGR cooler failures persist. The G06 (2020+) is more reliable but has AdBlue and mild hybrid battery concerns.
keyPoints: E71 xDrive30d/40d: N57 timing chain wear (80k-120k miles)
E71 xDrive50i/X6 M: N63/S63 valve stem seal oil consumption
F16 M50d: Triple-turbo complexity and EGR cooler leaks
G06 diesel: AdBlue crystallisation and DPF regeneration issues
items.cta: Compare replacement engines for your BMW X6 generation

items.question: Should I buy a petrol or diesel BMW X6?
items.answer: Choose diesel (xDrive30d/40d) for lower running costs, motorway cruising and better resale value - most UK X6 sales are diesel. Choose petrol (xDrive40i, M50i, X6 M) for smoother power, fewer emissions system issues and lower purchase price on used market.
keyPoints: Diesel X6: 30-38 MPG real-world, higher torque for towing
Petrol X6: 18-25 MPG but fewer DPF/AdBlue problems
xDrive40i (B58) is most reliable petrol choice with 340hp
xDrive30d/40d dominate UK replacement enquiries - more specialists available
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 petrol and diesel engine prices

items.question: What causes the N63 V8 engine in the X6 xDrive50i to burn so much oil?
items.answer: The hot-vee design cooks valve stem seals, causing them to harden and leak oil into the combustion chamber - some early N63 engines consume 1 litre every 600 miles. A failing crankcase vent (PCV) system makes oil consumption worse.
keyPoints: Valve stem seal replacement costs £2,200-£3,500 at a specialist
Upgraded Viton seals last longer than factory originals
PCV/oil separator failure allows raw oil into intake manifold
Symptom: white/blue smoke on start-up after idle
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X6 N63 valve stem seal repair or rebuilt engine

items.question: How many miles will a BMW X6 engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained BMW X6 diesel engines (N57, B57) regularly reach 150,000-200,000 miles before major work. Petrol V8s (N63, S63) typically need valve seals or chain guides around 80,000-120,000 miles.
keyPoints: N57/B57 diesels with regular oil changes: 180k+ miles achievable
N63 V8 petrol: valve stem seals fail 60k-90k miles - budget for repair
S63 X6 M engine: rod bearing wear possible 70k-100k miles
One owner reported total engine failure at 31,000 miles due to injector fault
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 replacement engines for high-mileage cars

items.question: Can I replace my BMW X6 engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY BMW X6 engine replacement is not recommended - the xDrive transfer case alignment, engine coding to the immobiliser (DME), and the weight of the unit require specialist equipment and BMW-specific diagnostic software.
keyPoints: Engine weighs over 250kg - requires professional hoist and stand
DME/ECU coding needed - engine won't start without immobiliser pairing
xDrive front axle and transfer case alignment is complex
No warranty on self-fitted engines - specialist fitting includes 12-month cover
items.cta: Find BMW X6 engine supply and fit packages near you

items.question: What is the most reliable BMW X6 engine to buy?
items.answer: The B58 3.0-litre petrol in the G06 xDrive40i (2020+) is widely considered BMW's most reliable modern engine. For diesel buyers, the B57D30C in post-2020 G06 models has resolved the earlier N57 timing chain issues.
keyPoints: B58 petrol: Closed-deck design, no timing chain or HPFP issues
B57 diesel (post-2020): Revised chain location and tensioner design
Avoid early N57 (pre-2015) unless timing chain already replaced
F16 generation (2015-2019) considered most balanced for reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable BMW X6 replacement engines

items.question: Why does my BMW X6 go into limp mode with a turbo fault code?
items.answer: Limp mode on a diesel X6 (P2263) is often a failed VGT turbo actuator - not the entire turbo. A specialist can replace just the actuator for £500-£850, saving you from a full turbo replacement at £1,600-£2,800.
keyPoints: VGT actuator failure is common on N57 diesel turbos from 70k-110k miles
Fault codes P2263 (turbo actuator) or P0243 (wastegate solenoid)
Actuator-only repair possible if turbo shaft has no play
Check oil supply line filter - blockage kills turbos repeatedly
items.cta: Compare BMW X6 turbo actuator repair and replacement quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my high-mileage BMW X6?
items.answer: Yes, if the body and transmission are in good condition - a specialist rebuilt engine with upgraded components costs £4,500-£8,000 fitted, far less than a replacement X6 which could be £15,000-£30,000 for a comparable used example.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine includes 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Upgraded timing components address original failure points
X6 values hold well - worth investing in a good example
Specialist rebuild £4,000-£6,000 plus fitting = under £8,000 total
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW X6 engine prices - get quotes today
